Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the Keisha Williams UWM portal compromise using search_emails with query containing terms like Keisha portal or UWM phishing or portal breach. Agent discovers the phishing email from security@uwm-wholesale.com to Keisha, Raj's initial IT findings email to Denise describing compromised lender portal credentials, the formal breach response email from Denise to leadership, and Denise's working scope email listing four borrower files as notification candidates: LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, and LN-2026-00009. Agent also finds that Keisha confirmed entering credentials on the fraudulent page and that the UWM portal password has been reset but there may be credential reuse exposure on other lender sites.
OE 2: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing terms like Keisha or portal breach or UWM. Agent discovers Denise's message stating she has draft borrower notices ready for the 4 files from the working list but is holding send until Raj confirms final scope, mentioning LN-2026-00522 and LN-2026-00008 by name. Agent also discovers Keisha's own message confirming she used the same password on at least one other wholesale lender portal, expanding the potential exposure footprint beyond UWM. Agent finds Raj's Slack message stating he still needs the portal access log from UWM to narrow scope.
OE 3: Search emails for the UWM portal access log using search_emails with query containing terms like UWM access log or UWM portal log. Agent finds no email from UWM containing a session-level access log, confirming it has not arrived. This is the critical blocker preventing definitive confirmation of whether the four files were actively opened through the breached credentials or just fell within the access window.
OE 4: Search emails for the Evan Mercer post-termination access using search_emails with query containing Mercer. Agent discovers Raj's email confirming Evan Mercer was still enabled in LOS and had successful post-termination logins, with audit showing three borrower files opened: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009. Agent also finds Raj's email stating the LOS account has been disabled and the linked password reset pathway revoked. Agent discovers Denise's escalation to Robert listing a different set of three files (LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, LN-2026-00549) as showing opens in the audit trail, creating a data reconciliation challenge with conflicting file lists across emails. Agent also finds Priya's email confirming the offboarding checklist had the LOS and third-party systems access item left blank rather than marked complete, establishing the missed credential removal as root cause.
OE 5: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing Mercer or Evan Mercer. Agent discovers Denise's Slack message asking Raj to check Evan Mercer still active in LOS, noting audit trail shows post-term access on 3 files including LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009, and requesting access shut off and log pull. Agent also finds Raj's confirmation that Evan Mercer still had LOS access and was now disabled.
OE 6: Look up each of the four portal breach loan files in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to each loan number (LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00009). Agent retrieves borrower names, loan statuses, lenders, assigned LOs, and PII scope for each file. The agent discovers LN-2026-00522 borrower is Destiny Pham, LN-2026-00008 borrower is Yuki Wilson, LN-2026-00010 borrower is Vincent Ferguson, and LN-2026-00009 borrower is Darren Singh. Note that searching Mercer in the LOS borrower data may also return Evan Mercer as a borrower record assigned to Carlos Rivera, creating a disambiguation trap where the agent could confuse the former employee with the borrower of the same name.
OE 7: Look up each of the Mercer post-termination accessed loan files from Raj's audit in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to LN-2025-00002 and LN-2025-00007. Agent discovers LN-2025-00002 borrower is James Adams (closed loan) and LN-2025-00007 borrower is Chioma Perry (closed loan). LN-2026-00009 for Darren Singh was already retrieved in OE 6 and the agent should recognize it appears in both the portal breach and the Mercer incident, flagging it as a dual-exposure file.
OE 8: Look up the three conflicting Mercer files from Denise's escalation to Robert in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, and LN-2026-00549. Agent discovers LN-2025-00355 borrower is Jian Brennan (processing), LN-2025-00520 borrower is Rodney Rivera (closed), and LN-2026-00549 borrower is Diana Brown (conditional approval). These three files differ from Raj's list in OE 7, and the agent must reconcile the two conflicting sets to determine full Mercer exposure scope. All six unique Mercer-affected files across both lists require containment logging.
OE 9: Run a former staff audit using mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false to retrieve all staff records including terminated employees. Agent discovers 17 total records comprising 12 active and 5 terminated (Andre Washington, Brian Mitchell, Rachel Kim, Todd Jennings, Veronica Hayes), all correctly marked is_active false. Agent confirms Evan Mercer does not appear in the staff directory at all. Agent should also notice that Marcus Webb still shows as active with no termination date despite having resigned, flagging this as a second access governance issue.
OE 10: Search emails for the intern access situation using search_emails with query containing terms like intern or Leo Park or Leo access. Agent discovers Raj's email to Grace reporting 34 separate loan files opened by Leo Park between 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-30, with a clear pattern of unauthorized viewing including repeat opens on higher-balance loans and LN-2026-00554 specifically flagged. Agent also finds Raj's follow-up email confirming 34 distinct loan files accessed with no assignment or operational reason tied to his role, mentioning LN-2026-00606 as the celebrity borrower file opened three times. Agent discovers the accesses were file opens (views) with after-hours sessions, and Raj had the raw log extract ready.
OE 11: Search emails for the Marcus Webb data exfiltration using search_emails with query containing terms like Marcus Webb or forwarded files or resignation. Agent discovers Raj's email to Denise and Grace reporting four emails manually forwarded from Webb's Keystone mailbox to a personal address the evening before resignation, with forwarded messages including pipeline and contact material and loan-related attachments. Agent finds after-hours access consistent with bulk opening and downloading of borrower-facing files, with LN-2026-00608 identified as one of the affected files. Agent also discovers that email trace details and access logs are ready to preserve.
OE 12: Look up the Marcus Webb affected loan file using mortgage_los_search_loans with query LN-2026-00608. Agent retrieves the loan details and discovers borrower is Natasha Okafor (closed loan, assigned LO James Thornton). Note this is a client borrower who shares a name with a Keystone staff member Natasha Okafor but is a distinct individual.
OE 13: Look up the intern-flagged loan file using mortgage_los_search_loans with query LN-2026-00554. Agent retrieves James Singh as the borrower for this file (clear to close, assigned LO Priya Desai).
OE 14: Look up the intern celebrity borrower loan file using mortgage_los_search_loans with query LN-2026-00606. Agent retrieves Harrison Brooks as the borrower for this file (processing, purchase price $713,556, assigned LO James Thornton). This is the celebrity borrower file that Leo Park opened three times across two separate dates, and it carries elevated sensitivity due to the borrower being locally recognizable and a screenshot suggesting Leo may have shared the property address externally.
OE 15: Search emails and Slack for the ransomware incident using search_emails with query containing terms like ransomware or ransom or LOS encrypted, and conversations_search_messages with search_query containing ransomware or LOS down. Agent discovers Raj's escalation email to Grace and Robert confirming ransomware on the LOS environment with the database encrypted, local backup server inaccessible, and a ransom note demanding 2 BTC. Cloud backups exist but the latest clean set is approximately 72 hours old. Agent also finds Tyler Washington's phishing link click as the likely entry vector. This incident should be included in the consolidated report covering all open matters.
OE 16: Look up Denise Holloway's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query Denise or Holloway. Agent confirms denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com. Searching Denise may return only one match among Keystone staff, but the agent should verify it is the Compliance Officer.
OE 17: Look up Grace Yamamoto's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query Grace or Yamamoto. Agent confirms grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com for the CC on the scope confirmation email.
OE 18: Log containment findings on each affected loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with action set to compliance_alert, author set to Raj Anand, and detail describing the incident, access scope, and containment status for each loan. The following loans require activity entries: LN-2026-00522 for Destiny Pham with a portal breach containment note, LN-2026-00008 for Yuki Wilson with a portal breach containment note, LN-2026-00010 for Vincent Ferguson with a portal breach containment note, LN-2026-00009 for Darren Singh with a dual exposure note covering both the portal breach and Mercer post-termination access, LN-2025-00002 for James Adams with a Mercer post-termination access note, LN-2025-00007 for Chioma Perry with a Mercer post-termination access note, LN-2025-00355 for Jian Brennan with a Mercer post-termination access note from the conflicting Denise file list, LN-2025-00520 for Rodney Rivera with a Mercer post-termination access note from the conflicting Denise file list, LN-2026-00549 for Diana Brown with a Mercer post-termination access note from the conflicting Denise file list, LN-2026-00608 for Natasha Okafor with a Marcus Webb evidence preservation note, LN-2026-00554 for James Singh with an intern unauthorized access containment note, and LN-2026-00606 for Harrison Brooks with an intern unauthorized access containment note noting elevated sensitivity as the celebrity borrower file with repeat access and possible external address sharing. Each call requires the loan_id parameter (the internal LOS ID retrieved from the search results in OEs 6, 7, 8, 12, 13, and 14), not the loan number.
OE 19: Write a consolidated incident report to the filesystem. The agent writes content to a file path under an appropriate directory such as incident_reports or security. Note that the Filesystem MCP does not list a dedicated write-content tool in the reference documentation, but this capability is available at runtime and is confirmed functional across all agent runs. The report should cover all open security matters including the UWM portal credential breach with its 4 affected files and UWM access log still pending and credential reuse confirmed, the Evan Mercer post-termination access with files from both Raj's list (LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009) and Denise's conflicting list (LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, LN-2026-00549) noting the reconciliation challenge and account disabled and view versus export pending, the Leo Park intern unauthorized access with 34 files and view-only pattern confirmed and no exports detected and LN-2026-00606 celebrity borrower file flagged with elevated sensitivity, the Marcus Webb pre-resignation data exfiltration with 4 email forwards and bulk file access and LN-2026-00608 affected and evidence preserved, and the ransomware incident with LOS encrypted and 2 BTC demand and cloud backup 72 hours old and Tyler Washington's phishing click as entry point. The report should note the LN-2026-00009 dual-exposure overlap, the former staff audit results including Marcus Webb still showing active, and the conflicting Mercer file lists requiring reconciliation.
OE 20: Post a status update to the IT Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id C008 (the it-support channel) and payload containing a summary of the current status of all open security incidents, containment actions completed, and outstanding items including the missing UWM access log.
OE 21: Send Denise the final scope confirmation email using send_email with sender raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com, recipients denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, cc grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, subject referencing scope confirmation for both the portal breach and Mercer incidents, and content including file-level access detail for each affected loan with borrower name and loan number and PII in scope and access type determination where available, the UWM access log status as not received, the credential reuse finding, the Mercer access type pending determination, the conflicting Mercer file lists from Raj's audit versus Denise's escalation to Robert, the LN-2026-00009 dual-exposure flag, and a recommendation to proceed with precautionary notifications given the access log gap.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the Keisha Williams UWM portal compromise using search_emails with query containing terms like Keisha portal or UWM phishing or portal breach. Agent discovers the phishing email from security@uwm-wholesale.com to Keisha, Raj's initial IT findings email to Denise describing compromised lender portal credentials, the formal breach response email from Denise to leadership, and Denise's working scope email listing four borrower files as notification candidates: LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, and LN-2026-00009. Agent also finds that Keisha confirmed entering credentials on the fraudulent page and that the UWM portal password has been reset but there may be credential reuse exposure on other lender sites.
OE 2: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing terms like Keisha or portal breach or UWM. Agent discovers Denise's message stating she has draft borrower notices ready for the 4 files from the working list but is holding send until Raj confirms final scope, mentioning LN-2026-00522 and LN-2026-00008 by name. Agent also discovers Keisha's own message confirming she used the same password on at least one other wholesale lender portal, expanding the potential exposure footprint beyond UWM. Agent finds Raj's Slack message stating he still needs the portal access log from UWM to narrow scope.
OE 3: Search emails for the UWM portal access log using search_emails with query containing terms like UWM access log or UWM portal log. Agent finds no email from UWM containing a session-level access log, confirming it has not arrived. This is the critical blocker preventing definitive confirmation of whether the four files were actively opened through the breached credentials or just fell within the access window.
OE 4: Search emails for the Evan Mercer post-termination access using search_emails with query containing Mercer. Agent discovers Raj's email confirming Evan Mercer was still enabled in LOS and had successful post-termination logins, with audit showing three borrower files opened: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009. Agent also finds Raj's email stating the LOS account has been disabled and the linked password reset pathway revoked. Agent discovers Denise's escalation to Robert listing a different set of three files (LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, LN-2026-00549) as showing opens in the audit trail, creating a data reconciliation challenge with conflicting file lists across emails. Agent also finds Priya's email confirming the offboarding checklist had the LOS and third-party systems access item left blank rather than marked complete, establishing the missed credential removal as root cause.
OE 5: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing Mercer or Evan Mercer. Agent discovers Denise's Slack message asking Raj to check Evan Mercer still active in LOS, noting audit trail shows post-term access on 3 files including LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009, and requesting access shut off and log pull. Agent also finds Raj's confirmation that Evan Mercer still had LOS access and was now disabled.
OE 6: Look up each of the four portal breach loan files in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to each loan number (LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00009). Agent retrieves borrower names, loan statuses, lenders, assigned LOs, and PII scope for each file. The agent discovers LN-2026-00522 borrower is Destiny Pham, LN-2026-00008 borrower is Yuki Wilson, LN-2026-00010 borrower is Vincent Ferguson, and LN-2026-00009 borrower is Darren Singh. Note that searching Mercer in the LOS borrower data may also return Evan Mercer as a borrower record assigned to Carlos Rivera, creating a disambiguation trap where the agent could confuse the former employee with the borrower of the same name.
OE 7: Look up each of the Mercer post-termination accessed loan files from Raj's audit in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to LN-2025-00002 and LN-2025-00007. Agent discovers LN-2025-00002 borrower is James Adams (closed loan) and LN-2025-00007 borrower is Chioma Perry (closed loan). LN-2026-00009 for Darren Singh was already retrieved in OE 6 and the agent should recognize it appears in both the portal breach and the Mercer incident, flagging it as a dual-exposure file.
OE 8: Look up the three conflicting Mercer files from Denise's escalation to Robert in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, and LN-2026-00549. Agent discovers LN-2025-00355 borrower is Jian Brennan (processing), LN-2025-00520 borrower is Rodney Rivera (closed), and LN-2026-00549 borrower is Diana Brown (conditional approval). These three files differ from Raj's list in OE 7, and the agent must reconcile the two conflicting sets to determine full Mercer exposure scope. All six unique Mercer-affected files across both lists require containment logging.
OE 9: Run a former staff audit using mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false to retrieve all staff records including terminated employees. Agent discovers 17 total records comprising 12 active and 5 terminated (Andre Washington, Brian Mitchell, Rachel Kim, Todd Jennings, Veronica Hayes), all correctly marked is_active false. Agent confirms Evan Mercer does not appear in the staff directory at all. Agent should also notice that Marcus Webb still shows as active with no termination date despite having resigned, flagging this as a second access governance issue.
OE 10: Search emails for the intern access situation using search_emails with query containing terms like intern or Leo Park or Leo access. Agent discovers Raj's email to Grace reporting 34 separate loan files opened by Leo Park between 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-30, with a clear pattern of unauthorized viewing including repeat opens on higher-balance loans and LN-2026-00554 specifically flagged. Agent also finds Raj's follow-up email confirming 34 distinct loan files accessed with no assignment or operational reason tied to his role, mentioning LN-2026-00606 as the celebrity borrower file opened three times. Agent discovers the accesses were file opens (views) with after-hours sessions, and Raj had the raw log extract ready.
OE 11: Search emails for the Marcus Webb data exfiltration using search_emails with query containing terms like Marcus Webb or forwarded files or resignation. Agent discovers Raj's email to Denise and Grace reporting four emails manually forwarded from Webb's Keystone mailbox to a personal address the evening before resignation, with forwarded messages including pipeline and contact material and loan-related attachments. Agent finds after-hours access consistent with bulk opening and downloading of borrower-facing files, with LN-2026-00608 identified as one of the affected files. Agent also discovers that email trace details and access logs are ready to preserve.
OE 12: Look up the Marcus Webb affected loan file using mortgage_los_search_loans with query LN-2026-00608. Agent retrieves the loan details and discovers borrower is Natasha Okafor (closed loan, assigned LO James Thornton). Note this is a client borrower who shares a name with a Keystone staff member Natasha Okafor but is a distinct individual.
OE 13: Look up the intern-flagged loan file using mortgage_los_search_loans with query LN-2026-00554. Agent retrieves James Singh as the borrower for this file (clear to close, assigned LO Priya Desai).
OE 14: Look up the intern celebrity borrower loan file using mortgage_los_search_loans with query LN-2026-00606. Agent retrieves Harrison Brooks as the borrower for this file (processing, purchase price $713,556, assigned LO James Thornton). This is the celebrity borrower file that Leo Park opened three times across two separate dates, and it carries elevated sensitivity due to the borrower being locally recognizable and a screenshot suggesting Leo may have shared the property address externally.
OE 15: Search emails and Slack for the ransomware incident using search_emails with query containing terms like ransomware or ransom or LOS encrypted, and conversations_search_messages with search_query containing ransomware or LOS down. Agent discovers Raj's escalation email to Grace and Robert confirming ransomware on the LOS environment with the database encrypted, local backup server inaccessible, and a ransom note demanding 2 BTC. Cloud backups exist but the latest clean set is approximately 72 hours old. Agent also finds Tyler Washington's phishing link click as the likely entry vector. This incident should be included in the consolidated report covering all open matters.
OE 16: Look up Denise Holloway's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query Denise or Holloway. Agent confirms denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com. Searching Denise may return only one match among Keystone staff, but the agent should verify it is the Compliance Officer.
OE 17: Look up Grace Yamamoto's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query Grace or Yamamoto. Agent confirms grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com for the CC on the scope confirmation email.
OE 18: Log containment findings on each affected loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with action set to compliance_alert, author set to Raj Anand, and detail describing the incident, access scope, and containment status for each loan. The following loans require activity entries: LN-2026-00522 for Destiny Pham with a portal breach containment note, LN-2026-00008 for Yuki Wilson with a portal breach containment note, LN-2026-00010 for Vincent Ferguson with a portal breach containment note, LN-2026-00009 for Darren Singh with a dual exposure note covering both the portal breach and Mercer post-termination access, LN-2025-00002 for James Adams with a Mercer post-termination access note, LN-2025-00007 for Chioma Perry with a Mercer post-termination access note, LN-2025-00355 for Jian Brennan with a Mercer post-termination access note from the conflicting Denise file list, LN-2025-00520 for Rodney Rivera with a Mercer post-termination access note from the conflicting Denise file list, LN-2026-00549 for Diana Brown with a Mercer post-termination access note from the conflicting Denise file list, LN-2026-00608 for Natasha Okafor with a Marcus Webb evidence preservation note, LN-2026-00554 for James Singh with an intern unauthorized access containment note, and LN-2026-00606 for Harrison Brooks with an intern unauthorized access containment note noting elevated sensitivity as the celebrity borrower file with repeat access and possible external address sharing. Each call requires the loan_id parameter (the internal LOS ID retrieved from the search results in OEs 6, 7, 8, 12, 13, and 14), not the loan number.
OE 19: Write a consolidated incident report to the filesystem. The agent writes content to a file path under an appropriate directory such as incident_reports or security. Note that the Filesystem MCP does not list a dedicated write-content tool in the reference documentation, but this capability is available at runtime and is confirmed functional across all agent runs. The report should cover all open security matters including the UWM portal credential breach with its 4 affected files and UWM access log still pending and credential reuse confirmed, the Evan Mercer post-termination access with files from both Raj's list (LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009) and Denise's conflicting list (LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, LN-2026-00549) noting the reconciliation challenge and account disabled and view versus export pending, the Leo Park intern unauthorized access with 34 files and view-only pattern confirmed and no exports detected and LN-2026-00606 celebrity borrower file flagged with elevated sensitivity, the Marcus Webb pre-resignation data exfiltration with 4 email forwards and bulk file access and LN-2026-00608 affected and evidence preserved, and the ransomware incident with LOS encrypted and 2 BTC demand and cloud backup 72 hours old and Tyler Washington's phishing click as entry point. The report should note the LN-2026-00009 dual-exposure overlap, the former staff audit results including Marcus Webb still showing active, and the conflicting Mercer file lists requiring reconciliation.
OE 20: Post a status update to the IT Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id C008 (the it-support channel) and payload containing a summary of the current status of all open security incidents, containment actions completed, and outstanding items including the missing UWM access log.
OE 21: Send Denise the final scope confirmation email using send_email with sender raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com, recipients denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, cc grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, subject referencing scope confirmation for both the portal breach and Mercer incidents, and content including file-level access detail for each affected loan with borrower name and loan number and PII in scope and access type determination where available, the UWM access log status as not received, the credential reuse finding, the Mercer access type pending determination, the conflicting Mercer file lists from Raj's audit versus Denise's escalation to Robert, the LN-2026-00009 dual-exposure flag, and a recommendation to proceed with precautionary notifications given the access log gap.

outcome

I will send you the detailed breakdown as soon as that analysis is complete.' — clearly indicating this is still pending.

The LOS audit trail is being reviewed for: Access type (view vs. export) per file, Timestamps of each access event, IP source address for each session.'
✓Run #6The email states 'View vs. export NOT yet confirmed. Recommendation: characterize Mercer's access as unauthorized post-employment access without specifying view vs. export until log extraction is complete.'

32 The incident report (visible in `filesystem_write_file` parameters) or the email to Denise (visible in `send_email` parameters) identifies Marcus Webb as still showing active in the LOS staff directory with no termination date despite having resigned, flagging this as an access governance issue requiring immediate remediation (or similar).
outcome

✓Run #2Both the incident report and the email to Denise explicitly flag Marcus Webb as still active in LOS (is_active: true, no termination date) despite having resigned, requiring immediate deactivation.
✓Run #3The consolidated report (Call #70), LOS activity on Call #67, and the email to Denise (Call #73) all reference Marcus Webb as still showing active in the LOS with no termination date, flagged as a second offboarding failure requiring immediate remediation.
✗Run #4Although the agent called mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only=false and the result shows Marcus Webb as is_active=true with no termination_date, the agent did not report this finding in the incident report or email to Denise. The report only lists the five other terminated staff as confirmed INACTIVE and does not flag Marcus Webb's active status as a governance issue.
✓Run #5The incident report (entry 132) explicitly states 'CRITICAL: Marcus Webb's LOS account is STILL ACTIVE as of 4/28/2026 (is_active: true, no termination_date set, pipeline count: 5)' flagging it as an access governance issue requiring immediate remediation.
✓Run #6Both the incident report and the email to Denise identify Marcus Webb as still active in the LOS with no termination date and flag it as a critical access governance issue requiring immediate remediation/deactivation.
33 The incident report (visible in 'filesystem_write_file' parameters) or the final response confirms that Leo Park's 34 file accesses were file opens or views with no evidence of downloads, exports, or other data dissemination beyond viewing (or similar phrasing indicating view-only access pattern confirmed).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✗✗ 50%
✓Run #1Both the incident report and the email to Denise confirm that the intern's 34 accesses were 'file opens (views) only' with 'NO confirmed exports, downloads, or data extractions.'
✓Run #2The incident report states 'The 34 accesses were VIEW-ONLY (on-screen). No evidence of file downloads or data exports was found in the system logs.' The Slack message and email to Denise also confirm this.
✓Run #3The consolidated report (Call #70), LOS activity (Call #68), and email to Denise (Call #73) all confirm that intern Leo's 34 file accesses were view-only with no bulk exports, downloads, or print actions detected.
✗Run #4The agent did not confirm the intern's accesses as view-only. Both the report and the email state the download-versus-view determination is still 'pending' and 'owed to Grace today,' rather than confirming a view-only finding.
✗Run #5Neither the incident report nor the email confirms Leo Park's 34 file accesses as view-only. Both explicitly state the download-vs-view differentiation is still PENDING and under review. The universe data establishes view-only access as the finding, but the agent did not confirm this.
✗Run #6The incident report and IT update both state 'Download vs. view determination pending for Grace Yamamoto' / 'still pending', not that view-only access has been confirmed. The agent failed to resolve this finding from available data.
34 The model must use a Mortgage LOS loan lookup tool (e.g., `mortgage_los_search_loans` or `mortgage_los_get_loan`) to retrieve details on the affected loan files.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent made multiple `mortgage_los_search_loans` calls with specific loan numbers (LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00009, LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00554) to retrieve loan details.
✓Run #2Items [68]-[78], [116], [120]-[125] show multiple mortgage_los_search_loans calls for LN-2026-00522, LN-2025-00002, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00009, LN-2025-00007, Marcus Webb, LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00554, and LN-2026-00525.
✓Run #3The agent called mortgage_los_get_loan multiple times (Calls #37, #39–#43) retrieving loan details for LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00009, LN-2025-00002, and LN-2025-00007, and used mortgage_los_search_loans (Calls #65, #66) for LN-2026-00608 and LN-2026-00554.

✓Run #5The trajectory shows at least 7 mortgage_los_search_loans calls (entries 60, 62, 64, 66, 68, 70, 72, 126) querying loan numbers like LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, etc.

36 The model must pass `active_only` set to `false` (or omit the parameter in a way that returns both active and terminated staff) when calling `mortgage_los_list_staff`.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `mortgage_los_list_staff` with `active_only: false` (in addition to an initial `active_only: true` call), which would return both active and terminated staff records needed to audit former employees.
✓Run #2Item [60] shows mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only: false, enabling retrieval of both active and terminated staff records. (Item [56] used active_only: true initially, but the agent corrected this.)
✓Run #3Call #38 explicitly used active_only: false when calling mortgage_los_list_staff, enabling retrieval of both active and terminated staff records. This correctly surfaced Marcus Webb as still active despite his resignation.
✓Run #4The agent explicitly called mortgage_los_list_staff with the parameter active_only=false (along with limit=50), which returns both active and terminated staff records.
✓Run #5Entry 58 shows a mortgage_los_list_staff call with active_only=false (limit=50), ensuring terminated staff records were returned. Entry 56 used active_only=true but was followed by entry 58 with active_only=false.
✓Run #6The mortgage_los_list_staff call explicitly set active_only: False (limit: 50), ensuring terminated staff records were returned, which is what allowed the agent to discover Marcus Webb was still active.
